disclaimer: Educational fitness content, not medical advice. Stop any movement that causes sharp pain, chest pain, dizziness, or shortness of breath, and clear new exercise with your doctor first — especially with heart, joint, blood-pressure, or pregnancy concerns.
clearance: Let's start this the safe way. Before your first workout, it's worth a quick word with your doctor — especially if you have a heart condition, diabetes, high blood pressure, joint pain, or you get chest pain, breathlessness, or dizziness when you're active. Most people get the green light to start with gentle walking. This takes 2 minutes and means you can move with total confidence. 💚
stop_signs: Breathlessness that feels severe or scary (not just “puffed”)
stop_note: If any of these don't settle quickly with rest, call your local emergency number. These are rare — knowing them just means you can move with confidence.
expectations: Here's the honest deal: what you eat drives the number on the scale — that's why SnapCal tracks it. Movement is what makes that weight loss healthy: it protects your muscle so you lose fat (not strength), guards your joints and heart, and lifts your mood and energy this week. Don't chase calories burned — chase how good you feel. 💪
global_rules: Warm up 3-5 min before and cool down/stretch 3-5 min after every session.
Breathe out on the effort, never hold your breath.
Move through a pain-free range only; 'challenging but doable' is the target, not 'maximum'.
Progress by adding reps first, then sets, then resistance — never all at once.
Use the Talk Test: if you can talk but not sing, you've nailed the right effort.

tiers.name: Start Here
tiers.tagline: Seated & standing-supported. Joint-friendly. No floor work.
tiers.audience: Heavy or very deconditioned beginners. Everything is done seated or standing while holding a sturdy chair, counter, or wall — nothing requires getting down to or up from the floor.
equipment: Counter or chair back for support
tiers.weekly: 3-5 days/week (start at 3). Strength days (A & C) + a mobility day (B), with short easy walks + desk-break snacks on the in-between days. Rest or an easy walk on weekends.

sessions.name: Session A — Full-Body Seated + Standing Strength

exercises.name: Seated Marching
exercises.cue: Sit tall, lift one knee then the other like a slow march, swinging arms gently.
exercises.easier: Lift each foot just an inch off the floor, or alternate small heel raises.

exercises.name: Seated Shoulder & Arm Circles
exercises.cue: Roll shoulders in slow, smooth circles; keep them relaxed, not shrugged.
exercises.easier: Tiny circles, or one shoulder at a time.

exercises.name: Sit-to-Stand (Chair Squat)
exercises.cue: Scoot forward, press through your heels, push hands on thighs if needed, stand fully, then sit slow.
exercises.easier: Push up using the armrests, or just lift your hips an inch before sitting back.

exercises.name: Wall Push-Ups
exercises.cue: Hands on wall at shoulder height, body straight, bend elbows to bring chest toward wall, press back.
exercises.easier: Stand closer to the wall (more upright = easier); fewer reps.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Extensions
exercises.cue: Sit tall, straighten one knee, squeeze the thigh, lower slowly.
exercises.easier: Extend only halfway, or alternate legs.

exercises.name: Seated Toe & Heel Raises
exercises.cue: Lift toes up, then heels up, rolling through the foot — keeps ankles and circulation working.
exercises.easier: Toes-only or heels-only, fewer reps.

sessions.cooldown: Seated neck side-bends, overhead reach, slow seated forward fold — hold each 15-20 sec.

sessions.name: Session B — Mobility & Gentle Range of Motion

exercises.name: Seated Neck Mobility
exercises.cue: Slow and small — ear toward shoulder, then look over each shoulder; never force or roll back.
exercises.easier: Reduce range to whatever is fully comfortable.

exercises.name: Seated Cat-Cow
exercises.cue: Hands on thighs; round the back and tuck chin, then arch and lift the chest — breathe with it.
exercises.easier: Smaller spinal movement.

exercises.name: Seated Torso Rotation
exercises.cue: Cross arms on chest, rotate gently to look behind you, return to center, other side.
exercises.easier: Smaller turn.

exercises.name: Seated Hamstring Stretch
exercises.cue: Extend one leg, heel on floor, sit tall and hinge slightly forward for a gentle stretch.
exercises.easier: Soft bend in the knee; lean less.

exercises.name: Seated Ankle Circles
exercises.cue: Lift one foot slightly and draw slow circles with the toes.
exercises.easier: Heel on the floor, circle just the front of the foot.

exercises.name: Standing Supported Calf Stretch
exercises.cue: Hold the counter, step one foot back with heel down, lean gently to stretch the calf.
exercises.easier: Smaller step back, or a seated calf press.

sessions.cooldown: Seated slow deep breathing × 5, shoulders dropping on each exhale.

sessions.name: Session C — Standing Strength & Balance (supported)

exercises.name: March in Place (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold the counter, march lifting knees to a comfortable height, tall posture.
exercises.easier: Smaller steps, or lift heels only; or seated marching.

exercises.name: Counter-Supported Mini Squats
exercises.cue: Hold the counter, feet hip-width, bend knees a few inches like sitting back, then stand.
exercises.easier: Bend only an inch or two; or sit-to-stands from a chair.

exercises.name: Standing Heel Raises (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold support, rise onto the balls of your feet, pause, lower slowly.
exercises.easier: Smaller lift; or seated with hands pressing on thighs.

exercises.name: Standing Side Leg Lift (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold support, body tall, lift one leg out to the side a few inches, lower with control.
exercises.easier: Lift only slightly; or seated knee-out openings.

exercises.name: Standing Leg-Back (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold support, squeeze the glute and press one leg straight back a few inches, don't arch.
exercises.easier: Small range; toes lightly brushing the floor.

exercises.name: Standing Weight Shifts (balance)
exercises.cue: Hold support, slowly shift weight side to side onto each foot.
exercises.easier: Smaller shifts, two hands on support.

sessions.cooldown: Standing supported calf stretch + seated hamstring stretch, 20 sec each.

tiers.name: Iron — Upper Body & Core
tiers.tagline: Gym strength to build muscle while you lose fat. Upper body + core, knee-safe.
tiers.audience: For when you're ready to lift. This builds and protects muscle so you lose FAT and stay solid — not skinny. Every move is upper-body or core, done seated, machine-based, or standing-supported to keep your knees safe: no squats, lunges, running, or jumping. Start lighter than you think and own the form first.
equipment: Machines if you have them (chest press, lat pulldown, seated row, cable)
A resistance band subs for any cable move
tiers.weekly: 2-3 non-consecutive days/week (e.g. Mon/Thu, or Mon/Wed/Fri). Leave at least a day between sessions that hit the same muscles. Keep your daily walks + desk-breaks going on top.
notes: Why this matters: lifting + high protein is your recomposition engine — you lose FAT while keeping and building muscle, so you finish solid, not saggy. This is your best defense against loose skin.
Form before weight, always. Start light, own the movement, and leave 1-2 reps 'in the tank' each set. Add a little weight only once every set is clean at the top of the rep range.
Knee-safe by design: upper-body and core only, seated/machine/supported. Breathe OUT on the effort — never hold your breath.

sessions.name: Push — Chest, Shoulders & Triceps

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press (or Dumbbell Bench Press)
exercises.cue: Seated, handles at chest height. Press smoothly to nearly-straight arms (don't lock hard), squeeze the chest, lower under control for 2-3 sec. Shoulders down and back.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, or wall/incline push-ups for 2 sets of as many as stay clean.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.cue: Back supported, dumbbells at ear height, press up and slightly together, lower to the ears. Brace your core, don't arch the low back.
exercises.easier: Lighter dumbbells, one arm at a time, or a seated band overhead press.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press or Machine Chest Fly
exercises.cue: Slight incline to hit the upper chest. Press/squeeze with control — feel the chest work, not the shoulders.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, fewer sets, or a band chest squeeze.

exercises.name: Cable or Band Triceps Pushdown
exercises.cue: Elbows pinned to your sides, push down to straight arms, squeeze the triceps, return slow. Only the forearms move.
exercises.easier: Lighter band, or seated overhead dumbbell extension.

exercises.name: Seated Overhead Dumbbell Triceps Extension
exercises.cue: One dumbbell in both hands overhead, lower behind your head with elbows pointing up, press back up. Slow and controlled.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, or a band triceps press.

sessions.cooldown: Doorway chest stretch 20-30 sec, overhead triceps stretch 20 sec/arm, slow shoulder rolls.

sessions.name: Pull — Back & Biceps

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (or Band Pulldown)
exercises.cue: Pull the bar to your upper chest by driving the elbows down and back, squeeze the shoulder blades, return slow. Lead with the back, not the arms.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, or a seated band pulldown / supported single-arm row.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row (or Dumbbell Bench Row)
exercises.cue: Tall chest, pull the handle to your belly, squeeze the shoulder blades together, control it back out. Don't rock with the low back.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, one arm at a time braced on a bench or your thigh.

exercises.name: Machine Reverse Fly or Band Pull-Apart
exercises.cue: Arms out to the sides, squeeze the shoulder blades, lead with the pinkies. Great for posture after a desk day.
exercises.easier: Lighter band, smaller range.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.cue: Elbows at your sides, curl without swinging, squeeze at the top, lower for a slow count of three.
exercises.easier: Lighter dumbbells, alternate arms, or band curls.

exercises.name: Cable or Band Face Pull
exercises.cue: Pull toward your forehead with elbows high, squeeze the rear shoulders. This is your posture move — undoes desk slump.
exercises.easier: Lighter band, seated.

sessions.cooldown: Seated lat stretch (reach overhead and lean) 20 sec/side, cross-body shoulder stretch 20 sec/arm, biceps wall stretch 20 sec/arm.

sessions.name: Core & Abs — Strong Middle

exercises.name: Seated Cable Crunch or Machine Crunch
exercises.cue: Curl your ribs toward your hips — round the spine, don't just bend at the hips. Exhale hard at the bottom and feel the abs squeeze.
exercises.easier: Band crunch anchored above you, or a seated lean-back hold.

exercises.name: Seated Knee Raises (chair or captain's chair)
exercises.cue: Sit tall, grip the seat, lift both knees toward your chest with control, lower slow without flopping. Lower abs do the work.
exercises.easier: One knee at a time, smaller lift, or braced seated marches.

exercises.name: Standing Cable/Band Pallof Press (anti-rotation)
exercises.cue: Band at chest height from the side, press straight out and resist the pull that wants to twist you. Brace the abs hard — builds a stable, protective core.
exercises.easier: Stand closer to the anchor (less resistance), fewer reps.

exercises.name: Incline or Counter Plank
exercises.cue: Hands on a sturdy counter or bench, body in one straight line, squeeze glutes and abs, breathe. No sagging hips — the higher your hands, the easier. No floor needed.
exercises.easier: Hands on a higher surface or the wall, shorter holds.

exercises.name: Standing Band Wood-Chop (gentle)
exercises.cue: Band low to one side, pull diagonally up and across with braced abs, control it back. Smooth, no jerking — obliques and rotation strength.
exercises.easier: Lighter band, smaller range, or do the Pallof press instead.

sessions.cooldown: Standing side-bend stretch 20 sec/side, gentle seated cat-cow ×5, deep breathing ×5.

desk_breaks.name: Desk-Break Snacks
desk_breaks.tagline: 2-5 minute movement breaks to interrupt long sitting.
desk_breaks.why: Breaking up 12-14 hours of sitting improves circulation, blood sugar, energy, posture, and hip/back comfort — more than one workout can offset alone.
desk_breaks.frequency: Take one every 30-60 minutes of sitting. Make the walk-around your longest, every 1-2 hours. Set a timer.

snacks.name: Stand & Reset
snacks.cue: Stand, roll shoulders back 5×, reach overhead, 3 slow breaths. Tall spine, shoulders down.

snacks.name: Calf Raises
snacks.cue: 20-30 standing calf raises by your desk — rise, squeeze, lower slow. Great for circulation.

snacks.name: Sit-to-Stands
snacks.cue: 10 stand-ups from your chair without using your hands. Press through the heels.

snacks.name: Hip-Flexor Stretch
snacks.cue: Standing lunge stretch, 30 sec per side. Tuck the tailbone, press the hip forward — undoes the 'seated hip'.

snacks.name: Posture Reset (Chin Tuck)
snacks.cue: Gently draw the chin straight back (double-chin) 5×; squeeze shoulder blades 5×. Counters 'tech neck'.

snacks.name: Neck & Shoulder Rolls
snacks.cue: 5 slow shoulder rolls back, 3 gentle neck side-bends each way. Release shoulders from the ears.

snacks.name: Walk-Around
snacks.cue: Walk to refill water, take a lap, or step outside. Bonus if you add stairs.

snacks.name: Marching in Place
snacks.cue: March in place 60-90 sec, pumping the arms — a light cardio snack to beat the afternoon dip.
